授業目的: The objectives of this course are:1. To develop students’ TOEIC listening skills2. To develop students’ TOEIC reading skills3. To help students build their vocabulary knowledge4. To help students improve their overall test-taking strategies5. To help students become aware of common distractors6. To help students better understand English as it is used in everyday work and life situations
到達目標: 1. Students will develop their listening and reading skills.2. Students will strengthen their vocabulary knowledge.3. Students will become better test-takers and more attentive to common distractors.4. Students will familiarize themselves with natural English.
関連科目: Practical English A (01-03)
授業方法:
1.
  This course will be conducted in English only and English will be the means
  of communication between the students and the instructor.  (Students will also be expected to
  communicate with one another in English.)
  2.  Students are encouraged to actively
  participate in class discussions, both independently and collaboratively, in
  order to enhance their competence and confidence in English and also to
  create a better learning environment for all.

成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: Students’ final grades will derive from a combination of assignments, activities, mini tests, etc.
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note      1.  Active participation is extremely important and counts as a significant part  of students’ evaluation marks.    2.  Attendance is crucial to students’ success in this course. The instructor  will carefully mark students’ attendance as present and on time, late, or absent.  Specific rules regarding attendance are as follows:   2a) Only students who are present at  the time the class begins will receive full attendance marks.   2b) Any student walking into the  classroom after the class has begun will be marked late. The instructor will  take this into account when giving students a participation grade.  2c) Being 15 to 29 minutes late will  equal half an absence (0.5).   2d) Each time students are late 30  minutes or more, they will be marked absent. However, they are still  encouraged to attend.    　　　2e) As a general rule, no more than three absences will  be permitted in this course.

name: 【200】Practical English C　２／Practical English C
成績評価備考:
備考　Note
1. Active participation is extremely important and counts as a significant part of students’ evaluation marks. 
2. Attendance is crucial to students’ success in this course. The instructor will carefully mark students’ attendance as present and on time, late, or absent. Specific rules regarding attendance are as follows:
2a) Only students who are present at the time the class begins will receive full attendance marks.
2b) Any student walking into the classroom after the class has begun will be marked late. The instructor will take this into account when giving students a participation grade.
2c) Being 15 to 29 minutes late will equal half an absence (0.5).
2d) Each time students are late 30 minutes or more, they will be marked absent. However, they are still encouraged to attend. 
2e) As a general rule, no more than three absences will be permitted in this course.
